rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrocatalytic nickel-catalyzed three-component carboacylation/silanoylation of alkenes using acyl chlorides and unactivated C(sp3)-H donors or hydrosilanes via Fe-mediated LMCT chlorine radical HAT
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation regenerates [FeIIICl4]- from [FeIICl3]- (reduced after LMCT/HAT); cathodic reduction of Ni(II) acyl intermediate F to Ni(I) species G enables turnover of the nickel cycle

key_intermediates.name: [FeIICl3]- (C) after LMCT loss of Cl radical
secondary alkyl radical after radical addition to acrylate (E)

electron_flow: Visible light excites [FeIIICl4]- which undergoes LMCT to release Cl• and [FeIICl3]-; Cl• abstracts a C(sp3)-H (or Si-H) to give a carbon (or silyl) radical D, while anodic oxidation of [FeIICl3]- with Cl- regenerates [FeIIICl4]-. In parallel, Ni(I) oxidatively adds to acyl chloride to give Ni(III)-acyl F, which is cathodically reduced to Ni(II)-acyl G; D adds to the alkene to give secondary radical E, which is captured by G to form Ni(III) alkyl-acyl H that reductively eliminates the ketone and regenerates Ni(I).
rate_determining_step: Not the C-H cleavage step (KIE kH/kD = 1.15); turnover-limiting step not explicitly assigned

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully suppressed with 3 equiv TEMPO; cyclohexyl-TEMPO adduct 47 detected by GCMS, supporting cyclohexyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: TBABClO4 (replace LiCl electrolyte)
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed, showing LiCl/chloride is essential for in situ [FeCl4]- formation

probe_reagents.name: Cyclohexyl chloride (in place of cyclohexane)
probe_reagents.observation: No target product, ruling out alkyl chloride as a key intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Ni(COD)2
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product, suggesting Ni(0) is not in the main catalytic cycle

probe_reagents.name: Preformed Ni(II) complex 48 (NiBr2·dtbbpy analog)
probe_reagents.observation: Stoichiometric: no product without photocatalyst; catalytic (10 mol%) gave 55% yield, supporting Ni(II) complex as a competent catalyst

probe_reagents.name: [D]-cyclohexane (d12-cyclohexane)
probe_reagents.observation: Parallel KIE kH/kD = 1.15, indicating C-H cleavage is not turnover-determining

notes: Dual catalytic cycle (Fe photoredox/LMCT + Ni cross-coupling) coupled to electrochemistry: anode regenerates Fe(III), cathode reduces Ni(III) to Ni(II)/Ni(I). CV data: Ni(II)/Ni(I) at -1.38 V and Ni(I)/Ni(0) at -1.88 V vs Fc/Fc+; Fe(II)/Fe(III) at -0.09 V vs Fc/Fc+. Hydrosilane variant proceeds analogously with Si-H HAT by Cl• to give silyl radical, then addition to alkene and Ni-mediated acylation.
